question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower calling supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with a CollateralSupply entry where assets==0 for a previously-zero collateral slot cause the collateralBitmap bit for that index to remain unset while the subsequent take increases debt, so that isHealthy iterates a bitmap missing that index and the post-take health check passes on an actually-undercollateralized position? Preconditions: market with N>=2 collaterals, borrower has zero collateral at index i, bundler called with collateralSupplies[0].assets==0 at index i followed by a sell take. Call sequence: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget(targetUnits, minSellerAssets, taker, receiver, [{i, 0, permit}], takes, 0, address(0)) -> Midnight.supplyCollateral(market, i, 0, taker) [bit NOT set] -> Midnight.take(...) [debt increases] -> isHealthy iterates bitmap [index i absent] -> SellerIsLiquidatable check. Invariant tested: collateralBitmap must reflect all indices with collateral > 0; isHealthy must not undercount maxDebt. Scoped impact: healthy-position liquidation or undercollateralized borrow accepted. Proof idea: fuzz assets==0 supply followed by debt-increasing take; assert isHealthy(market, id, taker) == true iff actual collateral-weighted maxDebt >= debt.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower calling repayAndWithdrawCollateral with a CollateralWithdrawal entry where assets==0 for a slot whose collateral is already zero cause clearBit to be skipped, leaving a phantom bit set in collateralBitmap, so that subsequent isHealthy and liquidate loops call IOracle.price() for a zero-collateral index and revert if that oracle is broken? Preconditions: borrower has collateral[i]==0 but collateralBitmap bit i is set (from a prior supply+full-withdraw sequence where clearBit was skipped), market has a reverting oracle at index i. Call sequence: repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, assets, onBehalf, permit, [{i, 0}], receiver, 0, address(0)) -> Midnight.withdrawCollateral(market, i, 0, onBehalf, receiver) [newCollateral==0, assets==0, clearBit NOT called] -> later isHealthy or liquidate iterates bitmap, hits bit i, calls oracle[i].price() -> revert. Invariant tested: bitmap must not contain bits for indices with zero collateral; oracle must not be called for zero-collateral slots. Scoped impact: fund freeze — valid liquidation or health check permanently reverts. Proof idea: unit test setting up phantom bit via zero-asset withdraw, then assert isHealthy reverts when oracle[i] reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker calling buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with collateralWithdrawals containing duplicate collateralIndex entries (same index twice, each withdrawing half the balance) cause the second withdrawCollateral call to clear the bit when collateral reaches zero, but if the first call already cleared the bit (impossible here) or leave the bit set after the first partial withdrawal, resulting in a bitmap that still marks the index active with zero collateral after both withdrawals complete? Preconditions: taker has collateral[i]==X, collateralWithdrawals==[{i, X/2}, {i, X/2}]. Call sequence: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> take (debt reduced) -> withdrawCollateral(i, X/2) [newCollateral==X/2>0, bit stays set] -> withdrawCollateral(i, X/2) [newCollateral==0, assets>0, clearBit called]. Invariant tested: after full withdrawal via two steps, bitmap bit must be cleared; no phantom bit. Scoped impact: bitmap corruption causing oracle DoS on subsequent health checks. Proof idea: fuzz split withdrawal amounts summing to full balance; assert collateralBitmap bit i == 0 after both calls.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower calling supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with collateralSupplies containing two entries for the same collateralIndex (index i) cause setBit to be called only on the first entry (when oldCollateral==0), and the second entry to silently add collateral without re-checking the MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER limit, potentially allowing more than 16 active collaterals if the second supply is for a different index that was already at zero? Preconditions: borrower already has 15 active collaterals, collateralSupplies has two new indices. Call sequence: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget -> supplyCollateral(i, assets1) [setBit, countBits==16, OK] -> supplyCollateral(j, assets2) [setBit, countBits==17, revert TooManyActivatedCollaterals]. Invariant tested: countBits(collateralBitmap) <= MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER at all times. Scoped impact: revert DoS or bitmap overflow if countBits check is bypassable. Proof idea: fuzz collateralSupplies length and indices near the 16-collateral limit; assert TooManyActivatedCollaterals is always thrown at the right boundary.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate (bitmap loop)] Can an unprivileged liquidator calling liquidate with a collateralIndex that is NOT set in the borrower's collateralBitmap (bit absent, collateral[collateralIndex]==0) but passing repaidUnits>0 cause liquidatedCollatPrice to remain 0 (never assigned in the bitmap loop), triggering a division-by-zero in seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, liquidatedCollatPrice), reverting the liquidation and freezing an unhealthy position? Preconditions: borrower is unhealthy, collateralIndex not in bitmap, repaidUnits>0, seizedAssets==0 input. Call sequence: liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 0, repaidUnits, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '') -> bitmap loop [collateralIndex not hit, liquidatedCollatPrice==0] -> seizedAssets = repaidUnits * lif / WAD * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / 0 -> revert. Invariant tested: unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable; liquidation must not revert due to bitmap/price state. Scoped impact: liquidation failure — unhealthy position cannot be liquidated. Proof idea: unit test with collateralIndex not in bitmap and repaidUnits>0; assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate (bitmap loop) / isHealthy] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit a bitmap state where a high-value collateral index bit is set but collateral[i]==0 (phantom bit) to make isHealthy return true (maxDebt += 0 * price == 0, no effect) while the liquidate loop calls IOracle(collateralParam.oracle).price() for that phantom index, and if that oracle returns a very large price, cause maxDebt to overflow uint256 in the liquidate loop, reverting the liquidation? Preconditions: phantom bit set at index i with collateral[i]==0, oracle[i].price() returns near type(uint256).max, borrower is unhealthy. Call sequence: liquidate -> bitmap loop -> maxDebt += 0 * maxPrice / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD [== 0, no overflow] -> badDebt -= 0 * maxPrice / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * WAD / maxLif [== 0]. Invariant tested: phantom bits must not cause arithmetic overflow or incorrect maxDebt/badDebt. Scoped impact: liquidation DoS or incorrect bad debt realization. Proof idea: fuzz oracle price at phantom-bit index; assert liquidate does not revert and maxDebt is correct.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy (bitmap loop)] Can an unprivileged attacker create a market with a collateral oracle that reverts, supply that collateral to a victim borrower's position (authorized), then trigger a phantom bit for that index so that every subsequent isHealthy call for the victim reverts, permanently freezing the victim's ability to withdraw other collateral or take new offers? Preconditions: attacker is authorized operator for victim, market has collateral[i] with reverting oracle, attacker calls supplyCollateral(i, 1) then withdrawCollateral(i, 1) [clearBit called, bit cleared] — actually this doesn't create phantom bit. Alternative: attacker supplies 1 wei, then victim's debt > 0, oracle reverts. Call sequence: supplyCollateral(i, 1, victim) [bit set] -> oracle[i] starts reverting -> isHealthy(market, id, victim) -> bitmap loop -> IOracle(oracle[i]).price() -> revert. Invariant tested: activated collateral with reverting oracle must not permanently freeze health checks. Scoped impact: fund freeze — victim cannot withdraw any collateral or take offers while in debt. Proof idea: unit test with reverting oracle at active collateral index; assert isHealthy reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / Midnight.take (isHealthy post-check)] Can an unprivileged borrower using supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget supply collateral at index i (bit set), then have a try/catch-skipped failed take leave the debt unchanged, then have a successful take increase debt, and then call withdrawCollateral(i, full_amount) in a separate transaction to clear the bit, leaving debt with no active collateral in the bitmap, making isHealthy return true (debt==0 check skipped since debt>0, but bitmap is empty so maxDebt==0 < debt) — wait, that would make isHealthy return false. Can the attacker instead clear the bit while keeping collateral>0? Preconditions: borrower has debt, calls withdrawCollateral(i, collateral[i]-1) [newCollateral==1>0, bit stays set], then calls withdrawCollateral(i, 1) [newCollateral==0, clearBit called] — health check passes at each step. Invariant tested: bitmap must be cleared exactly when collateral reaches zero; no path leaves collateral>0 with bit unset. Scoped impact: health check bypass enabling undercollateralized borrow. Proof idea: fuzz multi-step withdraw sequences; assert bit i is set iff collateral[i]>0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / clearBit logic] Can an unprivileged liquidator calling liquidate with seizedAssets input such that the computed newCollateral rounds to exactly 0 (seizedAssets == collateral[i]) cause clearBit to be called correctly, but if seizedAssets is computed from repaidUnits input and mulDivDown rounds seizedAssets down to collateral[i]-1 (leaving 1 wei), the bit stays set with 1 wei collateral, and a subsequent liquidation with seizedAssets==1 clears the bit — verify this is correct and no off-by-one leaves phantom bits? Preconditions: collateral[i]==X, repaidUnits chosen so seizedAssets = X.mulDivDown(lif,WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE,price) == X-1. Call sequence: liquidate(repaidUnits) -> seizedAssets==X-1 -> newCollateral==1 -> bit stays set -> liquidate(0, 1) -> newCollateral==0 -> clearBit. Invariant tested: clearBit is called iff newCollateral==0 after seizure; no phantom bits from rounding. Scoped impact: phantom bit causing oracle DoS on subsequent health checks. Proof idea: fuzz repaidUnits near full-collateral boundary; assert bit cleared iff collateral[i]==0 after liquidation.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate (bad debt path, seizedAssets==0 repaidUnits==0)] Can an unprivileged liquidator calling liquidate with both seizedAssets==0 and repaidUnits==0 (bad debt realization) on a borrower whose collateralBitmap has a phantom bit (bit set, collateral[i]==0) cause the bitmap loop to call oracle[i].price() unnecessarily, and if that oracle reverts, prevent bad debt realization entirely, leaving the market insolvent? Preconditions: borrower has phantom bit at index i, oracle[i] reverts, borrower has bad debt (badDebt>0). Call sequence: liquidate(market, anyIndex, 0, 0, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '') -> bitmap loop -> oracle[i].price() -> revert -> bad debt not realized. Invariant tested: bad debt realization must not be blocked by phantom bitmap bits; oracle must not be called for zero-collateral slots. Scoped impact: insolvency — bad debt accumulates without being socialized. Proof idea: unit test with phantom bit and reverting oracle; assert liquidate(0,0) reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.supplyCollateral / setBit] Can an unprivileged borrower calling Midnight.supplyCollateral directly (not via bundler) with collateralIndex==127 (maximum valid index for uint128 bitmap) on a market with only 1 collateral param (index 0) cause an array out-of-bounds revert when accessing market.collateralParams[127], preventing the supply and leaving the bitmap unchanged? Preconditions: market has collateralParams.length==1, borrower calls supplyCollateral(market, 127, assets, onBehalf). Call sequence: supplyCollateral -> touchMarket -> market.collateralParams[127] -> array OOB revert. Invariant tested: collateralIndex must be validated against market.collateralParams.length before bitmap operations. Scoped impact: revert DoS on supply; no bitmap corruption but confirms index validation is implicit. Proof idea: unit test with collateralIndex >= collateralParams.length; assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / Midnight.supplyCollateral / setBit] Can an unprivileged borrower calling supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with collateralSupplies listing indices in descending order (e.g., [2, 1, 0]) cause setBit to set bits in the correct positions regardless of supply order, and then the subsequent take's isHealthy loop (which iterates from MSB to LSB via msb+clearBit) correctly sums all three collaterals? Fuzz test: does the order of collateral supply in the bundler affect the final bitmap or health calculation? Preconditions: market with 3 collaterals, borrower supplies all three via bundler in reverse order. Call sequence: supplyCollateral(2, assets2) [bit 2 set] -> supplyCollateral(1, assets1) [bit 1 set] -> supplyCollateral(0, assets0) [bit 0 set] -> take -> isHealthy [iterates bits 2,1,0]. Invariant tested: bitmap correctly reflects all supplied collaterals regardless of supply order; isHealthy sums all active collaterals. Scoped impact: incorrect health calculation if order matters. Proof idea: fuzz supply order; assert isHealthy result is order-independent.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy / liquidate (msb loop)] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the msb+clearBit iteration pattern in isHealthy and liquidate to cause double-counting of a collateral if clearBit is not applied to the LOCAL copy of the bitmap (it is applied to _collateralBitmap local var, not _position.collateralBitmap) — verify that the local copy pattern prevents infinite loops and double-counting, and fuzz whether any code path modifies _position.collateralBitmap during the loop iteration in liquidate? Preconditions: borrower has N active collaterals, liquidate is called. Call sequence: liquidate -> _collateralBitmap = _position.collateralBitmap [local copy] -> loop: i=msb(_collateralBitmap), process, _collateralBitmap=clearBit(i) [local only] -> _position.collateralBitmap modified only in seize block after loop. Invariant tested: each collateral is counted exactly once in maxDebt/badDebt; no double-counting or skipping. Scoped impact: incorrect maxDebt causing wrong liquidatability determination. Proof idea: invariant test asserting sum of per-collateral contributions equals total maxDebt for any bitmap.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower calling repayAndWithdrawCollateral with collateralWithdrawals containing an entry where assets > collateral[i] cause an underflow revert in withdrawCollateral (uint128 subtraction), and does this revert propagate correctly to abort the entire bundler transaction, or does any try/catch in the bundler silently swallow the revert and leave the repay already executed but collateral not withdrawn? Preconditions: borrower has collateral[i]==X, collateralWithdrawals[0]=={i, X+1}. Call sequence: repayAndWithdrawCollateral -> repay(units) [debt reduced, withdrawable increased] -> withdrawCollateral(i, X+1) -> _position.collateral[i] - (X+1) underflows -> revert. Invariant tested: repay and collateral withdrawal must be atomic; partial execution must not leave debt reduced without collateral withdrawn. Scoped impact: accounting desync — debt reduced but collateral not freed. Proof idea: unit test with assets > collateral; assert entire transaction reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / Midnight.withdrawCollateral / isHealthy] Can an unprivileged taker calling buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral where the take loop uses try/catch (skipping failed takes) result in fewer units filled than expected, leaving the taker's debt higher than anticipated, and then the collateralWithdrawals loop withdraws collateral based on the original (higher) debt assumption, causing isHealthy to return false and withdrawCollateral to revert with UnhealthyBorrower, while the already-executed takes have modified the taker's position? Preconditions: taker has debt, some takes fail (caught), filledUnits < targetUnits -> revert OutOfOffers before withdrawals. Call sequence: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> take loop [some skipped] -> filledUnits < targetUnits -> revert OutOfOffers [withdrawals never reached]. Invariant tested: OutOfOffers revert must prevent partial collateral withdrawal on insufficient fill. Scoped impact: fund freeze if OutOfOffers revert leaves position in unexpected state. Proof idea: fuzz take failure rate; assert OutOfOffers reverts atomically.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / collateralBitmap loop / clearBit in seize block] Can an unprivileged liquidator calling liquidate on a borrower with multiple active collaterals (bitmap has bits 0,1,2 set) choose collateralIndex==1 for seizure, causing the seize block to clear bit 1 when newCollateral==0, while the bitmap loop (which ran before the seize block using the original bitmap) already counted collateral[1] in maxDebt — verify that the post-seizure bitmap correctly reflects only bits 0 and 2, and that a subsequent isHealthy call uses the updated bitmap? Preconditions: borrower has collateral at indices 0,1,2; liquidator seizes all of index 1. Call sequence: liquidate(collateralIndex=1, seizedAssets=collateral[1]) -> bitmap loop [counts 0,1,2] -> seize block [collateral[1]=0, clearBit(1)] -> _position.collateralBitmap now has bits 0,2 -> isHealthy [counts 0,2 only]. Invariant tested: post-liquidation bitmap must reflect only remaining non-zero collaterals. Scoped impact: incorrect post-liquidation health check if bitmap not updated. Proof idea: state test asserting collateralBitmap after liquidation equals pre-liquidation bitmap with seized index cleared.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.multicall / supplyCollateral / withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower using Midnight.multicall to sequence supplyCollateral(i, X) then withdrawCollateral(i, X) in a single transaction cause the bitmap to end up with bit i cleared (correct), but if the health check in withdrawCollateral passes because debt==0 (no oracle call), leave the bitmap in a state where bit i is cleared and collateral[i]==0, and then a subsequent supplyCollateral(i, 1) correctly sets the bit again? Fuzz: does multicall preserve bitmap invariants across supply+withdraw sequences? Preconditions: borrower has no debt, multicall with [supplyCollateral(i,X), withdrawCollateral(i,X)]. Call sequence: multicall -> supplyCollateral(i,X) [bit set] -> withdrawCollateral(i,X) [newCollateral==0, clearBit] -> bitmap bit i == 0. Invariant tested: bitmap is consistent after atomic supply+withdraw; no phantom bits. Proof idea: invariant test over all multicall supply/withdraw orderings.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / Midnight.take / isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower calling supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget where the collateralSupplies array is empty (length==0) and the takes array has sell offers cause the take to increase the taker's debt with no collateral supplied, and the post-take isHealthy check to use the existing (possibly empty) bitmap, potentially allowing debt increase with zero collateral if the taker already had sufficient collateral from a prior transaction? Preconditions: taker has existing collateral from prior supply, collateralSupplies==[], takes has sell offers. Call sequence: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget(targetUnits, minSellerAssets, taker, receiver, [], takes, 0, address(0)) -> no supplyCollateral calls -> take [debt increases] -> isHealthy [uses existing bitmap]. Invariant tested: debt increase must be backed by sufficient collateral; empty collateralSupplies is valid if existing collateral suffices. Scoped impact: undercollateralized borrow if existing bitmap is stale or corrupted. Proof idea: fuzz existing collateral state before bundler call; assert health invariant holds.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / bitmap loop / badDebt calculation] Can an unprivileged liquidator calling liquidate on a borrower with a phantom bit (bit set, collateral[i]==0) cause the badDebt calculation to be incorrect? badDebt = originalDebt - sum(collateral[i] * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * WAD / maxLif). With collateral[i]==0, the phantom bit contributes 0 to the subtraction, so badDebt is unchanged. Verify: does a phantom bit affect badDebt or only cause oracle revert risk? Preconditions: borrower has phantom bit at index i (collateral[i]==0, bit set), oracle[i] returns valid price. Call sequence: liquidate -> bitmap loop -> collateral[i]==0 -> maxDebt += 0, badDebt -= 0 -> no effect on values. Invariant tested: phantom bits must not inflate or deflate badDebt; bad debt socialization must be exact. Scoped impact: incorrect bad debt realization if phantom bit contributes non-zero value. Proof idea: differential test comparing liquidate with and without phantom bit; assert identical badDebt.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy / bitmap loop / multi-collateral valuation] Can an unprivileged borrower in a market with 16 active collaterals (MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER) cause isHealthy to skip the highest-index collateral if the msb function returns an index >= market.collateralParams.length, triggering an array out-of-bounds revert rather than a false return, effectively making the position appear permanently unhealthy (revert != false)? Preconditions: market has collateralParams.length==K, borrower has bit set at index K (impossible via supplyCollateral since it would revert). Verify: is it possible to set a bit at index >= collateralParams.length through any path? Call sequence: any path that sets bit at index >= collateralParams.length -> isHealthy -> market.collateralParams[i] OOB -> revert. Invariant tested: bitmap bits must only be set for valid collateral indices; isHealthy must not revert for valid positions. Scoped impact: fund freeze if revert prevents health check. [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral / Midnight.repay / withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower calling repayAndWithdrawCollateral with referralFeePct > 0 cause the units repaid (assets - referralFeeAssets) to be less than the borrower's full debt, leaving residual debt, and then the collateralWithdrawals attempt to withdraw all collateral, causing isHealthy to return false (residual debt > 0, collateral withdrawn) and revert with UnhealthyBorrower, while the repay has already been executed and loan tokens transferred? Preconditions: borrower has debt D, assets = D (intending full repay), referralFeePct > 0, so units = D - fee < D. Call sequence: repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, D, onBehalf, permit, [{i, collateral[i]}], receiver, feePct, feeRecipient) -> repay(D - fee) [debt = fee remaining] -> withdrawCollateral(i, collateral[i]) -> isHealthy [debt=fee>0, maxDebt=0] -> revert UnhealthyBorrower. Invariant tested: repayAndWithdrawCollateral must correctly account for referral fee when computing units to repay. Scoped impact: fund freeze — loan tokens transferred but collateral not freed. Proof idea: unit test with referralFeePct>0 and assets==debt; assert UnhealthyBorrower revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / bitmap loop / collateralIndex not in bitmap] Can an unprivileged liquidator calling liquidate with seizedAssets>0 and collateralIndex pointing to an index with collateral[collateralIndex]>0 but bit NOT set in collateralBitmap (hypothetical corruption) cause liquidatedCollatPrice==0 (never assigned in loop), then seizedAssets input path computes repaidUnits = seizedAssets * 0 / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * WAD / lif == 0, then newCollateral = collateral[collateralIndex] - seizedAssets (valid subtraction), clearBit NOT called (seizedAssets>0 but newCollateral may be 0), leaving the bitmap unchanged while collateral is seized? Preconditions: hypothetical state where collateral[i]>0 but bit i not set. Call sequence: liquidate(collateralIndex=i, seizedAssets=X, repaidUnits=0) -> loop skips i -> liquidatedCollatPrice==0 -> repaidUnits=0 -> newCollateral=collateral[i]-X -> clearBit if newCollateral==0. Invariant tested: liquidation must not seize collateral without repaying debt; repaidUnits must be > 0 for valid seizure. Scoped impact: unauthorized collateral seizure with zero debt repayment. Proof idea: construct hypothetical bitmap corruption state; assert repaidUnits > 0 whenever seizedAssets > 0.
